proven.claim: Reachability: when the asserted condition does NOT hold, the function must throw.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 111
; Reachability: when the asserted condition does NOT hold, the function must throw.
; Model the same transition; now we ask if a violating execution (no throw) is possible.
(declare-const cond Int)   ; 0 = false, 1 = true
(declare-const thrown Int) ; 0 = no‑throw, 1 = throw
; Function body transition
(assert (= thrown (ite (= cond 1) 0 1)))
; Assume the condition does NOT hold
(assert (= cond 0))
; Violation of the contract would be: the function does NOT throw (thrown = 0)
(assert (= thrown 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat (the model cond=0, thrown=0 satisfies all constraints, showing a
; reachable state that would break the contract if the function failed to throw.
; Since the real code sets thrown=1 for cond=0, this SAT result demonstrates that
; the *absence* of the throw is reachable only if the implementation is wrong.)
